div.style: color: rgb(232, 234, 240); display: inline-block; white-space: nowrap;
div.span: Ariel generates step-by-step diagram walkthroughs to help humans understand complex systems. It is most useful in READMEs and pull request summaries

path.style: fill: none; stroke: rgb(91, 141, 238) !important; stroke-width: 2.5px !important; stroke-dasharray: 8, 4 !important;

div.style: color: rgb(232, 234, 240); display: inline-block; white-space: nowrap;
div.span: LLMs generate code much faster than engineers. Once upon a time, our bottleneck was how fast we could write code. Now, the bottleneck is reviewing AI-generated code

path.style: fill: none; stroke: rgb(91, 141, 238) !important; stroke-width: 2.5px !important; stroke-dasharray: 8, 4 !important;

div.style: color: rgb(232, 234, 240); display: inline-block; white-space: nowrap;
div.span: While the code AIs generate is usually valid on a low-level, is still useful (perhaps critical) for human engineers to comprehend and evaluate the architecture.

path.style: fill: none; stroke: rgb(91, 141, 238) !important; stroke-width: 2.5px !important; stroke-dasharray: 8, 4 !important;

div.style: color: rgb(232, 234, 240); display: inline-block; white-space: nowrap;
div.span: Automatically generating diagrams helps. Now, instead of building a mental model from scratch, the reviewer has a map to cross-reference against the code.

path.style: fill: none; stroke: rgb(91, 141, 238) !important; stroke-width: 2.5px !important; stroke-dasharray: 8, 4 !important;
fill: none; stroke: rgb(91, 141, 238) !important; stroke-width: 2.5px !important; stroke-dasharray: 8, 4 !important;

div.style: color: rgb(232, 234, 240); display: inline-block; white-space: nowrap;
div.span: However, diagrams without narrative still leave reviewers context-switching — toggling between diagram and code, with no prescribed reading order.

path.style: fill: none; stroke: rgb(91, 141, 238) !important; stroke-width: 2.5px !important; stroke-dasharray: 8, 4 !important;
fill: none; stroke: rgb(91, 141, 238) !important; stroke-width: 2.5px !important; stroke-dasharray: 8, 4 !important;
fill: none; stroke: rgb(91, 141, 238) !important; stroke-width: 2.5px !important; stroke-dasharray: 8, 4 !important;

div.style: color: rgb(232, 234, 240); display: inline-block; white-space: nowrap;
div.span: Working memory has a hard ceiling. Overloaded reviewers rubber-stamp. Comprehending reviewers make better decisions.

path.style: fill: none; stroke: rgb(91, 141, 238) !important; stroke-width: 2.5px !important; stroke-dasharray: 8, 4 !important;
fill: none; stroke: rgb(91, 141, 238) !important; stroke-width: 2.5px !important; stroke-dasharray: 8, 4 !important;

div.style: color: rgb(232, 234, 240); display: inline-block; white-space: nowrap;
div.span: ariel sequences the narrative — one idea per step, one visual change per idea. Working memory stays within its limits. Understanding builds incrementally.
